data.title: Syndax Pharmaceuticals granted 138,900 shares to 10 new employees under the 2023 Inducement Plan on Oct. 1, 2025.
data.description: Syndax Pharmaceuticals, a biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol SNDX, revealed on October 1, 2025, that they provided 10 new employees with inducement awards. These awards allow the purchase of up to 138,900 shares of common stock. The vesting period for these stock options spans four years, with 25% of the shares vesting after one year from the commencement date, and the rest vesting monthly over the following 36 months. The employees must maintain their service at Syndax to receive the shares on the specified dates.

data.title: BAB, Inc. (OTCQB: BABB) releases financial results for Q3 FY 2025, ending on August 31, 2025, in Deerfield, Illinois.
data.description: In Deerfield, Illinois, on October 3, 2025, BAB, Inc. (OTCQB: BABB) released its financial report for the third quarter concluding on August 31, 2025.

data.title: QVC Group to discuss Q3 2025 results in conference call on November 5th at 8:30 a.m. E.T. (Nasdaq: QVCGA, QVCGP; OTCQB: QVCGB)
data.description: In West Chester, Pennsylvania, QVC Group, Inc. has announced a conference call to review the third-quarter 2025 results on November 5th at 8:30 a.m. E.T. The company, listed as QVCGA and QVCGP on Nasdaq and QVCGB on OTCQB, will provide insights before the market opens that day.

data.title: PR Newswire conducted a test on Oct. 3, 2025, with repeated phrases. No important numbers or details mentioned.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, a press release was issued from an unidentified source, repeating the phrase "This is a test from PR Newswire" multiple times. The purpose of this repetitive message was unclear, as it did not provide any substantial information or context. The message seemed to be a test from PR Newswire, possibly testing the distribution or formatting of their press releases. The exact number of times the phrase was repeated was not specified in the provided information.

data.title: Two WWII veterans, both over 100, recall camaraderie between China and the US during the war of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression.
data.description: In Guangzhou, China on October 3, 2025, a news report highlights the 80th anniversary of victory in the War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression. This significant milestone showcases the enduring bond established between Chinese and American servicemen during World War II. The commemoration reflects on the historical events and honors the shared experiences of individuals from both nations.

data.title: Curis, Inc. announces inducement grants under NASDAQ rule, focuses on emavusertib development. (Character count: 93)
data.description: On October 3, 2025, Curis, Inc., a biotechnology firm trading on NASDAQ under CRIS, based in Lexington, Massachusetts, disclosed their focus on emavusertib (CA-4948), an orally administered small molecule IRAK4 inhibitor. They revealed that on October 1, 2025, the independent Compensation Committee of the company made a significant announcement.

data.title: BridgeBio Pharma granted 23,884 shares of common stock to 11 new employees on Oct 2, 2025, under an Inducement Equity Plan.
data.description: In Palo Alto, California, on October 3, 2025, BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. (Nasdaq: BBIO) revealed that the compensation committee approved equity grants for 11 new employees in restricted stock units. The total number of shares granted is 23,884 of the Company’s common stock. Vesting details include one-fourth of the shares vesting on November 16, 2026, with one-twelfth of the remaining shares vesting quarterly thereafter. Vesting is contingent upon the employees' continuous employment with the Company or its subsidiaries on the vesting dates. These grants fall under BridgeBio’s Amended and Restated 2019 Inducement Equity Plan (the “Plan”).

data.title: Amex GBT and SAP Concur partner to innovate travel, expense, and payment experience.
data.description: A partnership has been formed between Amex Global Business Travel (GBT) and SAP Concur to revolutionize the business travel, expense, and payments sector. The alliance aims to enhance the overall experience in these areas. No specific details were provided regarding the terms of the partnership, but it is expected to have a significant impact on the industry. Stay tuned for more updates as this collaboration progresses.

data.title: Cosmos Health expands Sky Premium Life in $1Bn+ Kuwait market; secures initial order of 65,000+ units.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, Cosmos Health Inc. revealed that its subsidiary, SkyPharm S.A., signed an exclusive distribution deal on September 28, 2025, with Diyar United to distribute the Sky Premium Life® nutraceuticals line in Kuwait. Cosmos Health is a prominent healthcare group listed on NASDAQ under the symbol COSM, operating globally in a vertically integrated manner. This agreement marks a significant step for the Company's expansion, tapping into the Kuwaiti market.

data.title: ETH bulls are cautiously optimistic after a 17% weekly gain. Breaking $5,000 hinges on institutional accumulation amid competition and quiet derivatives.
data.description: The likelihood of Ether surpassing $5,000 hinges on institutional accumulation, with increasing competition and unremarkable derivatives metrics constraining investor confidence.

data.title: First Hawaiian, Inc. (NASDAQ: FHB) will report Q3 2025 financial results on Oct 24, 2025, with conference call at 1:00 p.m. ET (7:00 a.m. HT).
data.description: On October 3, 2025, First Hawaiian, Inc. revealed intentions to disclose its financial results for the third quarter of 2025 on October 24, 2025, before the market opens. The company, listed on NASDAQ as FHB, will hold a conference call on the same day at 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time (7:00 a.m. Hawaii Time) to discuss the results.

data.title: Arcutis Biotherapeutics grants 94,500 stock units to 12 new employees under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
data.description: Arcutis Biotherapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company in Westlake Village, California, announced the granting of a total of 94,500 restricted stock units of Arcutis’ common stock to 12 new employees. The awards were given the green light by the Compensation Committee of Arcutis’ Board of Directors and were issued under the Arcutis Biotherapeutics, Inc. 2022 Inducement Plan on October 1, 2025. This move was deemed crucial in enticing the new employees to join Arcutis, as per Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

data.title: Datavault AI to power Tokenized Dream Bowl XIV and Esports Championships on Jan. 11 at AT&T Stadium in TX, showcasing ADIO® and VerifyU® technologies.
data.description: Datavault's ADIO® and VerifyU® technologies will be featured in college football, Madden, team esports, and drone racing championships through partnerships with Cutting Edge Sports Management and FN Esports. These collaborations highlight Datavault's innovative technologies across various sports, benefitting athletes and enhancing the overall experience for fans.

data.title: Lawsuit challenges $100,000 H-1B visa fee, alleging harm to schools, hospitals, churches, and small businesses nationwide.
data.description: The lawsuit contends that the recently introduced fee would negatively impact various entities, including schools, hospitals, churches, and small businesses nationwide.

data.title: Eggo introduces limited-edition Stranger Things Strawberry Waffles, first-ever naturally-colored waffle, for fans to enjoy.
data.description: Fans will now have the chance to enjoy the delightful flavor of the brand's inaugural naturally-colored* Strawberry Waffle. This breakfast item is aimed to provide fans with the perfect fuel as they revisit the beloved Netflix series before its fifth and final season. The waffle is colored using sources from natural ingredients. Eggo has shaken up breakfast with this new offering, providing an exciting twist to the morning meal for its fans.

data.title: National Press Club President Mike Balsamo expresses concern over journalist Mario Guevara's deportation, highlighting threats to press freedom in the US.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, in Washington, the National Press Club expressed grave concerns regarding the expulsion of journalist Mario Guevara, stating that it sends a troubling message to media professionals in the United States. The incident highlights the issue of press freedom, as reporters may now be apprehensive about reporting the truth for fear of facing deportation. The removal of Guevara symbolizes a threat to journalistic independence and the free flow of information, impacting the ability of reporters to fulfill their duty to the public.
